item21_financials_patch.revenue_recognition_policy: ASC 606; ~50% of initial franchise fee at opening + training, ~50% straight-line over 10-year contract life; royalties recognized monthly under sales-based exception
item21_financials_patch.customer_concentration_flag: majority of A/R from one customer in both FY2024 and FY2023
item21_financials_patch.income_tax_structure: LLC member elected to be taxed as a corporation; no federal/state provision in entity statements; PTET paid at entity level on member's behalf

leadership_patch.related_party_governance_flag: Two of three master franchisees are also Cupbop directors: Kevin Santiago (Gold Bowl LLC, ~26 units) and Yong Min Kim (Cupbop Oklahoma, 3 units)

item17_relationship_patch.guaranty_basis: FA Exhibit H — unconditional, joint and several, primary and independent of franchisee liability; explicit waiver of statute of limitations and right to require pursuit of others; covers all 5%+ owners

unresolveds.description: Operating cash flow includes a $758,760 one-time related-party A/R collection (35.3% of operating cash flow). Sustainability of FY2024 operating cash flow without that swing is unclear.
Going-concern question: $21,352 of equity supports $636,503 of liabilities. Parent commitment to fund the franchising entity is undisclosed.

Cross-default scope: noncurable default lists 'any default under any other agreement with franchisor or affiliates.' Whether this includes franchisee obligations to affiliated supplier (Cupbop Co.) is unspecified.
Transfer ROFR mechanics not specified — how is 'matching offer' valued, what timeline.
FMV determination methodology on franchisor purchase option not specified
3 Las Vegas Gold Bowl closures in 2024 — market vs operator cause undisclosed
Marketing fund 40% admin overhead — peer benchmark comparison not in FDD
Customer concentration: majority of A/R from one customer (likely related party); identity not disclosed
All state effective dates (10 listed states) marked PENDING — none of the listed registration states is actually effective for sale at issuance date

California.forum_selection: Utah arbitration may not be enforceable; CA BPC §20040.5 controls
California.governing_law: Utah law may not apply; CA Franchise Investment Law and Franchise Relations Act control
California.termination: BPC §§20000–20043 control; bankruptcy termination may not be enforceable

Minnesota.forum_selection: litigation outside MN cannot be required; jury trial waiver invalid
Minnesota.anti_fraud: no acknowledgement waives claims including fraud-in-inducement

New York.disclosure_amendment: NY-mandated cover-page language; expanded Item 3 attestations re felony/antitrust/securities/fraud

Washington.forum_selection: WA arbitration site; WA litigation venue available
Washington.general_release: void except via post-effectiveness negotiated settlement with independent counsel (RCW 19.100.220(2)); also void on renewal/transfer
Washington.termination: franchisee may terminate under any state-law-permitted grounds
